full_text:
Bat falls off tree amid brutal UP heatwave, lawyers come to its aid in touching video
A bat that fell from a tree outside a court in Uttar Pradesh was helped by lawyers during the heatwave. The viral video has drawn attention to the toll of extreme temperatures on animals and birds.
As Uttar Pradesh continues to reel under an unforgiving heatwave, a heartfelt moment from a courtroom in Hamirpur has caught people’s attention online after lawyers stepped in to help an exhausted bat that fell from a tree due to the extreme heat.
The incident took place in Hamirpur district in Bundelkhand, where temperatures have crossed 46 degrees Celsius, leaving not just residents but also animals and birds struggling to cope with the scorching weather.
A video of the moment, now circulating widely on social media, showed a bat, believed to have fallen from a tree after being overwhelmed by the heat, lying weakly on the ground inside the court premises.
In the clip, lawyers present at the spot were seen gathering around the visibly distressed animal and attempting to help it recover. One of them carefully poured water from a bottle around the bat, creating a small wet patch to help cool it down in the intense heat.
Initially appearing weak and motionless, the bat slowly began responding. Moments later, it could be seen licking the water placed near it as the people around watched closely, hoping it would regain strength.
Watch the video here:
According to local accounts, after some time, the bat appeared noticeably more active and eventually recovered enough to fly away, disappearing back into the sky.
The incident come as several parts of Bundelkhand continue to battle severe heat conditions. With temperatures soaring past 46°C in parts of Uttar Pradesh, hot winds and relentless daytime heat have disrupted daily life, affecting both people and wildlife alike.
Reports of birds collapsing due to dehydration and heat exhaustion have also surfaced from multiple areas during the ongoing heatwave.
The viral clip has also sparked conversations online about the impact of extreme temperatures on animals and birds, with many praising the lawyers for stepping in and showing compassion toward a creature struggling to survive the heat.
Several users said the small act of kindness served as a reminder that rising temperatures are affecting every living being, not just humans.
